visa_policy.notes: Bolivia uses a 3-tier country grouping: Group I (no visa required for tourism), Group II (standard tourism visa required, 20 calendar days advance), Group III (visa with prior DIGEMIG consultation, 50-60 calendar days advance). Maximum stay for ALL tourists (including Group I) is 90 days per calendar year per Decreto Supremo 1923 Art. 9; the embassy EU page notes an initial 30-day stamp extendable twice. In December 2025, Bolivia's Foreign Ministry officially eliminated politically-motivated visa restrictions for 8 countries: USA, South Korea, South Africa, Bulgaria, Malta, Romania, UAE, and Israel (cancilleria.gob.bo/mre/2025/12/01/23577). The embajadadebolivia.eu page (crawled June 2026) has NOT been updated to reflect these December 2025 changes — it still lists South Korea in Group II and Israel in Group III; the cancilleria.gob.bo MFA announcement is the higher-authority and more recent source and takes precedence. Additional bilateral ordinary-passport visa-free agreements confirmed on cancilleria.gob.bo: Honduras (effective 17 March 2024), El Salvador (effective 21 June 2024), Dominican Republic (effective 2 September 2024). Russia: prior record included Russia in visa_free based on a 2016 bilateral agreement (cancilleria.gob.bo/mre/2016/04/12/8459); however, the embajadadebolivia.eu page (crawled June 2026) still lists Russia in Group II. Given the conflict between sources and the safety rule, Russia has been removed from visa_free and placed in data_quality.gaps pending re-verification against a current post-2022 official Bolivian source. MERCOSUR member nationals (Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay, Uruguay, Venezuela) and Mexico: no visa required for tourism; max stay 90 days per year per DS 1923. Andean Community members (Colombia, Ecuador, Peru) and Chile: 90-day visa-free entry. Visa-on-arrival for Group II nationals ended July 25, 2025 - Group II applicants must now obtain visas in advance. The official online visa application system is at visas.cancilleria.gob.bo. No e-visa or ETA system found on official sources. Chile-Bolivia agreement (March 2026) covers diplomatic/official passports only, does not affect ordinary-passport visa-free status already present in Group I.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of diplomatic, official, or service passports of any country may enter Bolivia without a visa. Specific bilateral agreements confirmed: Equatorial Guinea (signed 23 November 2017, cancilleria.gob.bo/mre/2017/11/23/8345), Switzerland (signed 7 December 2018, cancilleria.gob.bo/mre/2018/12/07/7883; stay duration linked to period of functions/assignment), Honduras (March 2024, covering diplomatic and official passports), Chile (effective March 9, 2026, cancilleria.gob.bo/mre/2026/03/09/24588/, covers diplomatic and official passports for official travel). The blanket 90-day rule for all diplomatic/official/service passports is widely reported by Timatic but has not been confirmed verbatim on a directly-accessed official Bolivian government page beyond these specific bilateral agreements.

cbi.notes: Bolivia has no official citizenship-by-investment (CBI) program. Bolivia's 2009 Constitution permits dual citizenship. Citizenship may be obtained after 3 years of continuous legal residency (standard naturalization path, not an investment program). No dedicated CBI program was found on any official Bolivian government source (migracion.gob.bo, cancilleria.gob.bo).

programs.name: Temporary Residency - Determined Purpose (Visa de Objeto Determinado) for Investors/Business
programs.notes: Foreign nationals who invest in Bolivian businesses, establish companies, or conduct business activities may apply for a 'Visa de Objeto Determinado' (Multiple Visa for investors/business). This visa is valid for 1 year with multiple entries; maximum 90-day stays per visit. After 2 years of temporary residency, MERCOSUR nationals can apply for permanent residency. After 3 years of continuous legal residency, any foreign national may apply for Bolivian citizenship. No minimum investment threshold is specified on official sources.

programs.name: Permanent Residency for MERCOSUR Nationals
programs.notes: Nationals of MERCOSUR member and associated states may apply for permanent residency after 2 years of valid temporary residence in Bolivia. Eligibility is governed by the MERCOSUR Residence Agreement. Bolivia formalized full MERCOSUR membership via Law 1567 (July 2024).

agreements: Andean Community (CAN) - free movement of persons under Decisions 503, 545, 878
MERCOSUR - free movement of persons; Bolivia formally acceded via Law 1567 (July 2024)

validation_notes: 2026-06-29: RE-VERIFIED Group I list via live crawl of embajadadebolivia.eu/en/turism-visa/ - original 49-country Group I list confirmed intact. Page has NOT been updated to reflect December 2025 changes (still lists South Korea in Group II and Israel in Group III).

2026-06-29: CONFIRMED December 2025 additions (USA, South Korea, South Africa, Bulgaria, Malta, Romania, UAE, Israel) via live re-fetch of cancilleria.gob.bo/mre/2025/12/01/23577/ - all 8 countries confirmed moved to Grupo I. The cancilleria.gob.bo MFA announcement is treated as the higher-authority and more recent source, taking precedence over the outdated embassy EU page.

2026-06-29: CONFIRMED Honduras, El Salvador, Dominican Republic bilateral ordinary-passport agreements via live re-fetch of individual cancilleria.gob.bo press releases - all confirmed valid with correct effective dates and passport-type coverage.

2026-06-29: REMOVED Russia (RUS) from visa_free. Prior record cited a 2016 bilateral agreement (cancilleria.gob.bo/mre/2016/04/12/8459). However, the embajadadebolivia.eu page (crawled live June 2026) still lists Russia under Group II (visa required). No current (post-2022) official Bolivian source was found confirming ongoing visa-free access for ordinary Russian passport holders. Per the safety rule (false visa-free = worst error), Russia is moved to data_quality.gaps pending re-verification.

2026-06-29: NOTED Chile-Bolivia agreement (effective March 9, 2026, cancilleria.gob.bo/mre/2026/03/09/24588/) covers diplomatic/official passports only. Chile was already in Group I for ordinary passports; no change to visa_free entry for Chile. Conditional access entry updated to include this agreement.

2026-06-29: Added note to Israel and South Korea entries documenting the conflict between the embajadadebolivia.eu page (outdated) and the December 2025 cancilleria.gob.bo announcement (authoritative), with clear rationale for which source takes precedence.

2026-06-27: CORRECTED max_stay_days for Argentina (ARG): was 180, now 90. Official DS 1923 Art. 9 (lexivox.org/norms/BO-DS-N1923.html) caps all tourist stays at 90 days per year. No official Bolivian source confirmed 180 days for MERCOSUR nationals in tourism context.
2026-06-27: CORRECTED max_stay_days for Brazil (BRA): was 180, now 90. Same basis as Argentina above.
2026-06-27: CORRECTED max_stay_days for Paraguay (PRY): was 180, now 90. Same basis as Argentina above.
2026-06-27: CORRECTED max_stay_days for Uruguay (URY): was 180, now 90. Same basis as Argentina above.
2026-06-27: CORRECTED max_stay_days for Venezuela (VEN): was 180, now 90. Same basis as Argentina above.
2026-06-27: CORRECTED max_stay_days for Mexico (MEX): was 180, now 90. DS 1923 Art. 9 caps all tourist stays at 90 days per year; no official source confirmed 180-day exception for Mexico.
ADDED Honduras (HND): Ordinary passport visa-free effective March 17, 2024, confirmed on cancilleria.gob.bo/mre/2024/03/16/14347.
ADDED Dominican Republic (DOM): Ordinary passport visa-free effective September 2, 2024, confirmed on cancilleria.gob.bo/mre/2024/08/30/17267.
ADDED El Salvador (SLV): Ordinary passport visa-free effective June 21, 2024, confirmed on cancilleria.gob.bo/mre/2024/06/18/15822.
NOTED: Visa-on-arrival for Group II nationals ended July 25, 2025 - all Group II applicants must now get visas in advance.
VERIFIED India (IND) as visa_required: India is classified in Grupo II; ordinary Indian passport holders must obtain a visa in advance.
gaps: Russia (RUS): Prior record had Russia as visa_free based on a 2016 bilateral agreement (cancilleria.gob.bo/mre/2016/04/12/8459). The embajadadebolivia.eu page (crawled live June 2026) lists Russia under Group II (visa required). No current post-2022 official Bolivian government source was found confirming ongoing visa-free access for ordinary Russian passport holders. Russia requires re-verification against a current (2024-2026) official Bolivian source before being re-admitted to visa_free. Until then, classified as visa_required per the safety rule.

Maximum stay days for newly visa-exempt countries (US, Korea, UAE, Israel, Bulgaria, Malta, Romania, South Africa) not explicitly stated in the December 2025 cancilleria.gob.bo announcement; 90 days per year assumed consistent with DS 1923 Art. 9.
Suriname: Listed as visa-free in Wikipedia aggregation of Bolivia's visa policy but no official Bolivian government source was found confirming this; omitted pending official confirmation.

MERCOSUR nationals (Argentina, Brazil, Paraguay, Uruguay, Venezuela) and Mexico max_stay_days: 90 days per DS 1923. The embassy EU page states '30 days extendable twice' for Group I generally; whether MERCOSUR/CAN nationals receive a per-entry stamp of 30 or 90 days is unresolved - DS 1923 sets the calendar-year cap at 90 days but does not specify per-entry stamp length for each group.

CONDITIONAL ACCESS - blanket diplomatic/official/service passport rule (90-day, all countries): widely reported by Timatic but not confirmed verbatim on a directly-accessed official Bolivian government page beyond specific bilateral agreements.

CONDITIONAL ACCESS - no official Bolivian government source was found granting visa-free access based on holding a US, Schengen, UK, Canadian, Australian, or other third-country visa or residence permit.
CONDITIONAL ACCESS - no OCI or analogous diaspora/heritage special status rule found for Bolivia on any official source.
No official minimum investment amount found for the investor/business residency path on official government sources.
No digital nomad visa, startup visa, or fast-track talent program found on official sources.
San Marino not listed explicitly on the embassy EU page; omitted due to lack of official confirmation.

Group II and Group III country lists: the embajadadebolivia.eu page predates multiple 2024 bilateral agreements and the December 2025 changes. The post-2025 Group II/III lists have not been re-verified on a current official source that explicitly lists all countries.

Turkey and Philippines remain in Group I per embajadadebolivia.eu (confirmed in June 2026 live crawl); their inclusion has not been independently re-verified against a separately-maintained current official Bolivian source.

outbound_official.note: No official Bolivia government outbound visa-list page located. Bolivia's Cancillería (cancilleria.gob.bo) publishes individual press releases for each new bilateral visa-suppression agreement but maintains no single authoritative page listing all countries where Bolivian ordinary-passport holders can travel without a prior consular visa. The visa-application portal visas.cancilleria.gob.bo and the servicios/visas pages all address inbound (foreigners entering Bolivia) requirements only.
